National Insurance (Non-participation—Assurance of Equivalent Pension Benefits) Regulations 1960
items.description: The National Insurance Act, 1959, provides that where, at the end of a person's service in a non-participating employment, he is not assured of equivalent pension benefits in respect of the period of his service, his employer must make a payment in lieu of contributions to the National Insurance Fund.

The National Insurance (Graduated Contributions and Non-participating Employments–Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1960
items.description: These Regulations contain in Part II provisions as to the payment of graduated and weekly contributions in the case of persons employed in both participating and non-participating employments and as to the calculation in such cases of the excess graduated contributions which, under the National Insurance Act, 1959, are refundable and do not count for benefit.
